---
title: "Shared Resources"
subtitle: "Brand design, prompts, and API patterns used by both platforms"
---

## Overview

Both the macOS Swift app and the Expo mobile app share common design language, prompts, and API integration patterns. This section documents the shared foundations.

## What's Shared

| Resource | macOS | Mobile | Purpose |
|----------|-------|--------|---------|
| Brand colors | `Brand.swift` (NanoDesignKit) | `colors.ts` | KaosMaps palette |
| Yoda prompt | `Constants.swift` | `constants.ts` | Copyright-safe prompt |
| Gemini API | `GeminiClient.swift` (URLSession) | `geminiClient.ts` (fetch) | REST API client |
| Error handling | `GeminiAPIError.swift` | `GeminiAPIError` class | PROHIBITED_CONTENT |
| Text overlay | CoreGraphics renderer | AI prompt-based | Meme text |

## Architecture Parallel

```{mermaid}
graph TB
    subgraph macOS [macOS Swift]
        SC[GeminiClient.swift] --> SM[GeminiModels.swift]
        IG[ImageGenerator.swift] --> SC
        IG --> TO[TextOverlayRenderer]
    end

    subgraph Mobile [Expo React Native]
        TC[geminiClient.ts] --> TT[geminiTypes.ts]
        MS[memeService.ts] --> TC
        MS --> AI[AI Prompt Text]
    end

    API[Gemini REST API] --> SC
    API --> TC
```

Both platforms call the same Gemini REST endpoint, use the same Codable/TypeScript types, and handle the same error codes. The key difference is text overlay: macOS renders text via Core Graphics post-generation, while mobile includes text in the AI prompt.

## Pages

- [Brand Design & Color Palette](brand-design.qmd) — KaosMaps colors with interactive palette explorer
- [Gemini API Reference](gemini-api.qmd) — Shared API patterns across platforms
